content.text: Answer four problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problem.text: Let \(R\) be a nonzero commutative ring with identity. Show that \(R\) has a minimal prime ideal, i.e. a prime ideal not containing any other prime ideal.
Which of the following polynomials are irreducible? Explain.
Show that a nonzero ring \(R\) with identity (not necessarily commutative) is a division ring if and only if its only left ideals are \(0\) and \(R\).

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Define what it means for an element of \(R\) to be irreducible.

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Define what it means for an element of \(R\) to be prime.

subparts.label: (iii)
subparts.text: Show that a prime element is irreducible.

problem.text: Suppose \(F\) is a field and \(V\) is an \(F\)-vector space. Denote by \(V'\) the dual of \(V\), i.e. the \(F\)-vector space of linear maps \(V\to F\).

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Show that if \(\dim_F V<\infty\), then \(V\simeq V'\).

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Show that if \(V\) has infinite dimension, then \(V\not\simeq V'\).

problem.text: Show that if \(L/K\) and \(E/L\) are algebraic extensions of fields, then so is \(E/K\).
Show that \(GL_3(\mathbb{F}_2)\) has two conjugacy classes of elements of order \(7\), and find representatives of each.
